InstructionsForUse.IndicationsForUse: For monitoring SpO2 and pulse rate in adult patients (18 years and older) with a physician-diagnosed respiratory condition placing them at risk of hypoxemia, in home and ambulatory non-clinical settings, as an adjunct to clinical judgment and physician-directed care.

Para el control de la SpO2 y la frecuencia del pulso en pacientes adultos (18 años o más) con una afección respiratoria diagnosticada por un médico que conlleve riesgo de hipoxemia, en entornos domésticos y ambulatorios no clínicos, como complemento del criterio clínico y la atención dirigida por un médico.

Warnings.Warning: Do not use as the sole basis for diagnosis or treatment decisions.
Do not use during transport, surgery, or in critical care environments.
Seek immediate medical attention if you experience symptoms of low oxygen, regardless of the device reading.
Nail polish, artificial nails, or poor peripheral circulation may cause inaccurate readings.

Precautions.Precaution: Ensure the sensor is fully seated on a clean, dry finger.
Keep your phone within 10 meters (33 feet) of the device to maintain Bluetooth connection.
Rotate finger placement periodically during extended monitoring sessions.

StepByStepInstructions.Step: Charge PulseTrack fully via the included USB-C cable.
Download and open the PulseTrack companion app; pair via Bluetooth.
Clip the sensor onto a clean, dry fingertip.
Wait 10 seconds for the reading to stabilize.
If a "low signal quality" indicator appears, reposition the sensor.
Configure the low-SpO2 alert threshold as directed by your healthcare provider.

InstructionsForUse.Maintenance: Wipe the sensor with a soft cloth dampened with 70% isopropyl alcohol between uses. Do not immerse in water. Do not autoclave.
